FROM M3AP-Constants;
-- **************************************************************
--
-- Interface Elementary Procedure Class
--
-- **************************************************************
M3AP-ELEMENTARY-PROCEDURE ::= CLASS {
 &InitiatingMessage ,
 &SuccessfulOutcome OPTIONAL,
 &UnsuccessfulOutcome OPTIONAL,
 &procedureCode ProcedureCode UNIQUE,
 &criticality Criticality DEFAULT ignore
}
WITH SYNTAX {
 INITIATING MESSAGE &InitiatingMessage
 [SUCCESSFUL OUTCOME &SuccessfulOutcome]
 [UNSUCCESSFUL OUTCOME &UnsuccessfulOutcome]
 PROCEDURE CODE &procedureCode
 [CRITICALITY &criticality]
}
-- **************************************************************
--
-- Interface PDU Definition
--
--
 INITIATING MESSAGE MBMSSessionStartRequest
 SUCCESSFUL OUTCOME MBMSSessionStartResponse
 UNSUCCESSFUL OUTCOME MBMSSessionStartFailure
 PROCEDURE CODE id-mBMSsessionStart
 CRITICALITY reject
}
mBMSsessionStop M3AP-ELEMENTARY-PROCEDURE ::= {
 INITIATING MESSAGE MBMSSessionStopRequest
 SUCCESSFUL OUTCOME MBMSSessionStopResponse
 PROCEDURE CODE id-mBMSsessionStop
 CRITICALITY reject
}
mBMSsessionUpdate M3AP-ELEMENTARY-PROCEDURE ::= {
 INITIATING MESSAGE MBMSSessionUpdateRequest
 SUCCESSFUL OUTCOME MBMSSessionUpdateResponse
 UNSUCCESSFUL OUTCOME MBMSSessionUpdateFailure
 PROCEDURE CODE id-mBMSsessionUpdate
 CRITICALITY reject
} 
errorIndication M3AP-ELEMENTARY-PROCEDURE ::= {
 INITIATING MESSAGE ErrorIndication
 PROCEDURE CODE id-errorIndication
 CRITICALITY ignore
}
reset M3AP-ELEMENTARY-PROCEDURE ::= {
 INITIATING MESSAGE Reset
 SUCCESSFUL OUTCOME ResetAcknowledge
 PROCEDURE CODE id-Reset

reasoning_content:
The function `MCE_handle_MBMS_SESSION_START_REQUEST` is not actually parsing the incoming M3AP PDU. It's just creating and sending a generic ITTI message to the MCE application. According to the ASN.1 specification, the MBMSSessionStartRequest includes several mandatory IEs (Information Elements) and optional IEs. 

Looking at the ASN.1 spec, the `MBMSSessionStartRequest-IEs` includes:
- Mandatory: id-MME-MBMS-M3AP-ID, TMGI, MBMS-E-RAB-QoS-Parameters, MBMS-Session-Duration, MBMS-Service-Area, MinimumTimeToMBMSDataTransfer, TNL-Information
- Optional: id-MBMS-Session-ID, id-Time-ofMBMS-DataTransfer, id-Reestablishment, id-Alternative-TNL-Information, id-MBMS-Cell-List

The issue states there is a "missing optional field that is conditionally mandatory per 3GPP specification". This suggests that while a field is marked as "optional" in the ASN.1 spec, there are conditions under which it becomes mandatory.

Looking at the optional fields:
- `id-MBMS-Session-ID` - CRITICALITY ignore
- `id-Time-ofMBMS-DataTransfer` - CRITICALITY ignore  
- `id-Reestablishment` - CRITICALITY ignore
- `id-Alternative-TNL-Information` - CRITICALITY ignore
- `id-MBMS-Cell-List` - CRITICALITY reject

The comment says "downstream of function MCE_handle_MBMS_SESSION_START_REQUEST" is where the missing field is conditionally mandatory. This suggests that the issue might not be in the function itself, but in the processing that happens after this function sends the message to TASK_MCE_APP.
